# accepts

[![NPM Version][npm-version-image]][npm-url]
[![NPM Downloads][npm-downloads-image]][npm-url]
[![Node.js Version][node-version-image]][node-version-url]
[![Build Status][travis-image]][travis-url]
[![Test Coverage][coveralls-image]][coveralls-url]

Higher level content negotiation based on [negotiator](https://www.npmjs.com/package/negotiator). Extracted from [koa](https://www.npmjs.com/package/koa) for general use.

In addition to negotiator, it allows:

- Allows types as an array or arguments list, ie `(['text/html', 'application/json'])` as well as `('text/html', 'application/json')`.
- Allows type shorthands such as `json`.
- Returns `false` when no types match.
- Treats non-existent headers as `*`.

## Installation

```sh
$ npm install accepts
```

## API

```js
var accepts = require('accepts')
```

### accepts(req)

Create a new `Accepts` object for the given `req`.

#### .types(types)

Return the first accepted type (and it is returned as the same text as what the user provided), or `false` if nothing in `types` is accepted.

- `types` is an array or arguments list of acceptable types.

```js
// Accept: text/html
accepts(req).types(['json', 'text']); // => "text"
accepts(req).types('json', 'text'); // => "text"

// Accept: application/json
accepts(req).types('json'); // => "json"
accepts(req).types('html', 'json'); // => "json"

// Accept: text/*, application/json
accepts(req).types('image/png'); // => false
accepts(req).types('png'); // => false
```

For the exact behavior, see the [examples in the test suite](https://github.com/jshttp/accepts/blob/master/test/types.js).

#### .encodings(encodings)

Return the first accepted encoding (and it is returned as the same text as what the user provided), or `false` if nothing in `encodings` is accepted.

- `encodings` is an array or arguments list of acceptable encodings.

```js
// Accept-Encoding: gzip
accepts(req).encodings(['gzip', 'deflate']); // => "gzip"
accepts(req).encodings('gzip', 'deflate'); // => "gzip"

// Accept-Encoding: gzip, deflate
accepts(req).encodings('gzip'); // => "gzip"
accepts(req).encodings(); // => ["gzip", "deflate", "identity"]
accepts(req).encodings(null); // => ["gzip", "deflate", "identity"]
```

When no `Accept-Encoding` header is present, `identity` is assumed to be acceptable, but if the client explicitly sends `identity;q=0`, then no encoding is acceptable.

#### .charsets(charsets)

Return the first accepted charset (and it is returned as the same text as what the user provided), or `false` if nothing in `charsets` is accepted.

- `charsets` is an array or arguments list of acceptable charsets.

```js
// Accept-Charset: utf-8, iso-8859-1;q=0.2, utf-7;q=0.5
accepts(req).charsets(); // => ["utf-8", "utf-7", "iso-8859-1"]
accepts(req).charsets('utf-8', 'utf-7'); // => "utf-8"
accepts(req).charsets('utf-16'); // => false
```

#### .languages(languages)

Return the first accepted language (and it is returned as the same text as what the user provided), or `false` if nothing in `languages` is accepted.

- `languages` is an array or arguments list of acceptable languages.

```js
// Accept-Language: en;q=0.8, es, pt
accepts(req).languages(); // => ["es", "pt", "en"]
accepts(req).languages('es', 'en'); // => "es"
accepts(req).languages(['en', 'es']); // => "es"
accepts(req).languages('fr'); // => false
```

## Examples

### Express

```js
var accepts = require('accepts')
var express = require('express')

var app = express()

app.get('/', function (req, res) {
  var accept = accepts(req)

  // the order of this list is significant; should be server preferred order
  switch (accept.type(['json', 'html'])) {
    case 'json':
      res.json({ hello: 'world' })
      break
    case 'html':
      res.send('<p>Hello, world!</p>')
      break
    default:
      // the fallback is text/plain, so no need to specify it above
      res.send('Hello, world!')
      break
  }
})

app.listen(3000)
```
